name: esp8266_deauther

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with jayofelony/pwnagotchi (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with evilsocket/pwnagotchi (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with worknshubh/wifi-sukuna (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: Scan for WiFi devices, block selected connections, create dozens of networks and confuse WiFi scanners.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Scan for WiFi devices, block selected connections, create dozens of networks and confuse WiFi scanners.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `esp8266_deauther` from its upstream repository, then run `esp8266_deauther --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. Scan for WiFi devices, block selected connections, create dozens of networks and confuse WiFi scanners.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Wireless, use esp8266_deauther because Scan for WiFi devices, block selected connections, create dozens of networks and confuse WiFi scanners.
When chaining Wireless tools, pair esp8266_deauther with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `esp8266_deauther --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
